title: berkheya carlinopsis
intro: Berkheya carlinopsis, also known as the "Bergkrans" or "Wishbone Bush", is a flowering plant species within the daisy family Asteraceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Berkheya carlinopsis include the potential treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in water, which can be used as a tea or applied topically as a poultice to treat various ailments.
